title: ‘Spider-Man: No Way Home’ Is Here!
format: A three-way, eventually-spoiler-filled review of the Spider-Man multiverse crossover, then Sean talks to Tommy Oliver about his Juice WRLD documentary.

segments.heading: ‘Spider-Man: No Way Home’
segments.note: Sean Fennessey, Amanda Dobbins, and Mallory Rubin

films.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
films.note: All three loved it. Mallory did a full rewatch of every prior Spider-Man film to prepare; Amanda, no fan of comic-book movies generally, calls it “completely delightful”; Sean says it's one of his best theater experiences in five years. Aunt May dies, and Peter ends the movie having every person in his life forget he's Spider-Man, including MJ and Ned.

films.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
films.note: The John Watts-directed start of the Tom Holland run — lighter, John Hughes-ish, set mostly in a Queens high school.

films.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
films.note: Sean argues it's heavier than it gets credit for: the Infinity Saga's coda, dealing with the fallout of Tony Stark's death and Peter handing off Edith to Mysterio.

films.title: Captain America: Civil War
films.note: Tom Holland's Peter Parker made his MCU debut here, before his own standalone film.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: No Way Home picks up in the instant Far From Home ends, itself downstream of Endgame.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Amanda's comparison point for MCU mythology that didn't land for her — a new cast, fake Greek mythology, and a robot she couldn't buy into.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: A good movie, per Sean, let down by a noisy, messy CGI ending.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Sean says he still doesn't totally understand the last 30 minutes.

films.title: Thor: The Dark World
films.note: The other MCU “convergence” event, raised as a contrast to how much smaller-scale this movie's universe-opening actually is.

segments.heading: Three Spider-Men, the Villains, and What's Next

films.title: Daredevil
films.note: Ben Affleck's version, which Sean calls “deeply not good.” Comes up because Charlie Cox's Matt Murdock cameo confirms the Netflix Daredevil now exists inside the MCU proper.

films.title: Spider-Man 3
films.note: The cautionary tale for too many villains crowding one movie — a trap this one avoids despite juggling five.

films.title: The Amazing Spider-Man
films.note: Sean's one defense of the Garfield era — he likes this one, not the sequel.

films.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
films.note: The other too-many-villains cautionary tale, and part of why Garfield only got two films before the franchise was rebooted again.

films.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
films.note: Raised in the Miles Morales conversation — the animated franchise everyone agrees is great, with two more entries expected before Miles shows up in live action.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: The trailer that plays after the credits, directed by Sam Raimi, with a look at Wanda, America Chavez, and Shuma-Gorath.

segments.heading: Tommy Oliver on ‘Juice WRLD: Into the Abyss’

films.title: Juice WRLD: Into the Abyss
films.note: HBO's final Music Box documentary. Built from a dumped hard drive of 8,000 clips and roughly 250 hours of footage, told almost entirely through Juice WRLD's own freestyles and archival tape, with sit-down interviews limited to people who were actually in his life at the time.

films.title: 40 Years a Prisoner
films.note: Oliver's prior documentary, which premiered at Toronto and is what led to the Ringer/HBO pitch for this project.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Oliver's pick for the last great thing he's seen — he praises its ambition and an ending built on one-on-one stakes rather than MCU-scale spectacle.

TV: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier

Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Ring.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings

Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madn.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
